title: Area-deprivation deep-dive (SVI vs. ADI vs. tract poverty)
context: BACKLOG: The core analysis uses area-based SES (SVI or census-tract poverty) as a dimension, but the choice of measure matters for defensibility and framing. CDC/ATSDR SVI, the Area Deprivation Index (ADI), and census-tract poverty/income each have different coverage, validity, and stigmatization risks. This research task evaluates the options and recommends the least-stigmatizing, most-defensible measure for the chosen cancer site and partner context.
objective: Research and evaluate the three main area-based socioeconomic measures used in cancer disparity analysis — CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index (SVI), the Area Deprivation Index (ADI), and census-tract poverty/income — against criteria including: data coverage and availability for the exemplar site, validity and peer-review basis for cancer disparity research, stigmatization risk (is the measure framed as exposure/environment vs. individual behavior?), alignment with the project's framing policy, and partner interpretability. Recommend the most defensible, least-stigmatizing measure (or combination) for the exemplar site and document the rationale.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluates SVI, ADI, and census-tract poverty/income on: data coverage for the exemplar site, peer-review basis for cancer disparity research, geographical availability at the required granularity without unacceptable suppression

Evaluates each measure's stigmatization risk: is it framed as an area-level exposure/environment, or does it risk being read as an individual-behavior proxy? Which framing is least stigmatizing and most consistent with disparities-analysis-framing-001?
Evaluates partner interpretability: which measure will be most legible and useful to the target delivery partner (COE office, coalition, public-health department)?
Recommends the most defensible, least-stigmatizing measure (or justified combination) with documented rationale scored against the evaluation criteria
Recommendation is reviewed and approved by the methods reviewer and framing reviewer
The chosen measure is documented for use in the core analysis module and the recommendation is cited in subsequent disparity analyses that include area-SES as a dimension
output: A research document evaluating SVI vs. ADI vs. census-tract poverty for cancer disparity analysis and recommending the most defensible, least-stigmatizing area-SES measure for the exemplar site — reviewed by methods and framing reviewers.
